DtRes_explanation: The phrase 'would not have died' indicates a change of state (death) that did not occur, implying the resulting state (not dead) would hold at the reference time.
DtUniv_explanation: The phrase 'would not have died' refers to a counterfactual past event and does not express a continuous state or habitual series from a past starting point up to the reference time.
TLAspPfv_explanation: The phrase 'would not have died' presents the event of dying as a completed, bounded whole, focusing on the single completed event of death rather than its internal temporal development.
